speaker: KING
text: Now, lords, if God doth give successful end

speaker: KING
text: To this debate that bleedeth at our doors,

speaker: KING
text: We will our youth lead on to higher fields

speaker: KING
text: And draw no swords but what are sanctified.

speaker: KING
text: Our navy is addressed, our power collected,

speaker: KING
text: Our substitutes in absence well invested,

speaker: KING
text: And everything lies level to our wish.

speaker: KING
text: Only we want a little personal strength;

speaker: KING
text: And pause us till these rebels now afoot

speaker: KING
text: Come underneath the yoke of government.

speaker: WARWICK
text: Both which we doubt not but your Majesty

speaker: WARWICK
text: Shall soon enjoy.

speaker: KING
text: Humphrey, my son of Gloucester, where is the Prince your brother?

speaker:
HUMPHREY
               
              OF
               
              GLOUCESTER
text: I think he’s gone to hunt, my lord, at Windsor.

speaker: KING
text: And how accompanied?

speaker:
HUMPHREY
               
              OF
               
              GLOUCESTER
text: I do not know, my lord.

speaker: KING
text: Is not his brother Thomas of Clarence with him?

speaker:
HUMPHREY
               
              OF
               
              GLOUCESTER
text: No, my good lord, he is in presence here.

speaker:
THOMAS
               
              OF
               
              CLARENCE
text: What would

speaker:
THOMAS
               
              OF
               
              CLARENCE
text: my lord and father?

speaker: KING
text: Nothing but well to thee, Thomas of Clarence.

speaker: KING
text: How chance thou art not with the Prince thy brother?

speaker: KING
text: He loves thee, and thou dost neglect him, Thomas.

speaker: KING
text: Thou hast a better place in his affection

speaker: KING
text: Than all thy brothers. Cherish it, my boy,

speaker: KING
text: And noble offices thou mayst effect

speaker: KING
text: Of mediation, after I am dead,

speaker: KING
text: Between his greatness and thy other brethren.

speaker: KING
text: Therefore omit him not, blunt not his love,

speaker: KING
text: Nor lose the good advantage of his grace

speaker: KING
text: By seeming cold or careless of his will.

speaker: KING
text: For he is gracious if he be observed;

speaker: KING
text: He hath a tear for pity, and a hand

speaker: KING
text: Open as day for melting charity;

speaker: KING
text: Yet notwithstanding, being incensed he is flint,

speaker: KING
text: As humorous as winter, and as sudden

speaker: KING
text: As flaws congealèd in the spring of day.

speaker: KING
text: His temper therefore must be well observed.

speaker: KING
text: Chide him for faults, and do it reverently,

speaker: KING
text: When you perceive his blood inclined to mirth;

speaker: KING
text: But, being moody, give him time and scope

speaker: KING
text: Till that his passions, like a whale on ground,

speaker: KING
text: Confound themselves with working. Learn this, Thomas,

speaker: KING
text: And thou shalt prove a shelter to thy friends,

speaker: KING
text: A hoop of gold to bind thy brothers in,

speaker: KING
text: That the united vessel of their blood,

speaker: KING
text: Mingled with venom of suggestion

speaker: KING
text: (As, force perforce, the age will pour it in),

speaker: KING
text: Shall never leak, though it do work as strong

speaker: KING
text: As aconitum or rash gunpowder.

speaker:
THOMAS
               
              OF
               
              CLARENCE
text: I shall observe him with all care and love.

speaker: KING
text: Why art thou not at Windsor with him, Thomas?

speaker:
THOMAS
               
              OF
               
              CLARENCE
text: He is not there today; he dines in London.

speaker: KING
text: And how accompanied? Canst thou tell that?

speaker:
THOMAS
               
              OF
               
              CLARENCE
text: With Poins and other his continual followers.

speaker: KING
text: Most subject is the fattest soil to weeds,

speaker: KING
text: And he, the noble image of my youth,

speaker: KING
text: Is overspread with them; therefore my grief

speaker: KING
text: Stretches itself beyond the hour of death.

speaker: KING
text: The blood weeps from my heart when I do shape,

speaker: KING
text: In forms imaginary, th’ unguided days

speaker: KING
text: And rotten times that you shall look upon

speaker: KING
text: When I am sleeping with my ancestors.

speaker: KING
text: For when his headstrong riot hath no curb,

speaker: KING
text: When rage and hot blood are his counsellors,

speaker: KING
text: When means and lavish manners meet together,

speaker: KING
text: O, with what wings shall his affections fly

speaker: KING
text: Towards fronting peril and opposed decay!

speaker: WARWICK
text: My gracious lord, you look beyond him quite.

speaker: WARWICK
text: The Prince but studies his companions

speaker: WARWICK
text: Like a strange tongue, wherein, to gain the language,

speaker: WARWICK
text: ’Tis needful that the most immodest word

speaker: WARWICK
text: Be looked upon and learned; which, once attained,

speaker: WARWICK
text: Your Highness knows, comes to no further use

speaker: WARWICK
text: But to be known and hated. So, like gross terms,

speaker: WARWICK
text: The Prince will, in the perfectness of time,

speaker: WARWICK
text: Cast off his followers, and their memory

speaker: WARWICK
text: Shall as a pattern or a measure live,

speaker: WARWICK
text: By which his Grace must mete the lives of others,

speaker: WARWICK
text: Turning past evils to advantages.

speaker: KING
text: ’Tis seldom when the bee doth leave her comb

speaker: KING
text: In the dead carrion.

speaker: KING
text: Who’s here? Westmoreland?

speaker: WESTMORELAND
text: Health to my sovereign, and new happiness

speaker: WESTMORELAND
text: Added to that that I am to deliver.

speaker: WESTMORELAND
text: Prince John your son doth kiss your Grace’s hand.

speaker: WESTMORELAND
text: Mowbray, the Bishop Scroop, Hastings, and all

speaker: WESTMORELAND
text: Are brought to the correction of your law.

speaker: WESTMORELAND
text: There is not now a rebel’s sword unsheathed,

speaker: WESTMORELAND
text: But peace puts forth her olive everywhere.

speaker: WESTMORELAND
text: The manner how this action hath been borne

speaker: WESTMORELAND
text: Here at more leisure may your Highness read

speaker: WESTMORELAND
text: With every course in his particular.

speaker: KING
text: O Westmoreland, thou art a summer bird,

speaker: KING
text: Which ever in the haunch of winter sings

speaker: KING
text: The lifting up of day.

speaker: KING
text: Look, here’s more news.

speaker: HARCOURT
text: From enemies heavens keep your Majesty,

speaker: HARCOURT
text: And when they stand against you, may they fall

speaker: HARCOURT
text: As those that I am come to tell you of.

speaker: HARCOURT
text: The Earl Northumberland and the Lord Bardolph,

speaker: HARCOURT
text: With a great power of English and of Scots,

speaker: HARCOURT
text: Are by the shrieve of Yorkshire overthrown.

speaker: HARCOURT
text: The manner and true order of the fight

speaker: HARCOURT
text: This packet, please it you, contains at large.

speaker: KING
text: And wherefore should these good news make me sick?

speaker: KING
text: Will Fortune never come with both hands full,

speaker: KING
text: But write her fair words still in foulest letters?

speaker: KING
text: She either gives a stomach and no food—

speaker: KING
text: Such are the poor, in health—or else a feast

speaker: KING
text: And takes away the stomach—such are the rich,

speaker: KING
text: That have abundance and enjoy it not.

speaker: KING
text: I should rejoice now at this happy news,

speaker: KING
text: And now my sight fails, and my brain is giddy.

speaker: KING
text: O, me! Come near me, now I am much ill.

speaker:
HUMPHREY
               
              OF
               
              GLOUCESTER
text: Comfort, your Majesty.

speaker:
THOMAS
               
              OF
               
              CLARENCE
text: O, my royal father!

speaker: WESTMORELAND
text: My sovereign lord, cheer up yourself, look up.

speaker: WARWICK
text: Be patient, princes. You do know these fits

speaker: WARWICK
text: Are with his Highness very ordinary.

speaker: WARWICK
text: Stand from him, give him air. He’ll straight be well.

speaker:
THOMAS
               
              OF
               
              CLARENCE
text: No, no, he cannot long hold out these pangs.

speaker:
THOMAS
               
              OF
               
              CLARENCE
text: Th’ incessant care and labor of his mind

speaker:
THOMAS
               
              OF
               
              CLARENCE
text: Hath wrought the mure that should confine it in

speaker:
THOMAS
               
              OF
               
              CLARENCE
text: So thin that life looks through and will break out.

speaker:
HUMPHREY
               
              OF
               
              GLOUCESTER
text: The people fear me, for they do observe

speaker:
HUMPHREY
               
              OF
               
              GLOUCESTER
text: Unfathered heirs and loathly births of nature.

speaker:
HUMPHREY
               
              OF
               
              GLOUCESTER
text: The seasons change their manners, as the year

speaker:
HUMPHREY
               
              OF
               
              GLOUCESTER
text: Had found some months asleep and leapt them over.

speaker:
THOMAS
               
              OF
               
              CLARENCE
text: The river hath thrice flowed, no ebb between,

speaker:
THOMAS
               
              OF
               
              CLARENCE
text: And the old folk, time’s doting chronicles,

speaker:
THOMAS
               
              OF
               
              CLARENCE
text: Say it did so a little time before

speaker:
THOMAS
               
              OF
               
              CLARENCE
text: That our great-grandsire, Edward, sicked and died.

speaker: WARWICK
text: Speak lower, princes, for the King recovers.

speaker:
HUMPHREY
               
              OF
               
              GLOUCESTER
text: This apoplexy will certain be his end.

speaker: KING
text: I pray you take me up and bear me hence

speaker: KING
text: Into some other chamber. Softly, pray.

speaker: KING
text: Let there be no noise made, my gentle friends,

speaker: KING
text: Unless some dull and favorable hand

speaker: KING
text: Will whisper music to my weary spirit.

speaker: WARWICK
text: Call for the music in the other room.

speaker: KING
text: Set me the crown upon my pillow here.

speaker:
THOMAS
               
              OF
               
              CLARENCE
text: His eye is hollow, and he changes much.

speaker: WARWICK
text: Less noise, less noise.

speaker: PRINCE
text: Who saw the Duke of Clarence?

speaker:
THOMAS
               
              OF
               
              CLARENCE
text: I am here, brother, full of heaviness.

speaker: PRINCE
text: How now, rain within doors, and none abroad?

speaker: PRINCE
text: How doth the King?

speaker:
HUMPHREY
               
              OF
               
              GLOUCESTER
text: Exceeding ill.

speaker: PRINCE
text: Heard he the good news yet? Tell it him.

speaker:
HUMPHREY
               
              OF
               
              GLOUCESTER
text: He altered much upon the hearing it.

speaker: PRINCE
text: If he be sick with joy, he’ll recover without

speaker: PRINCE
text: physic.

speaker: WARWICK
text: Not so much noise, my lords.—Sweet prince, speak low.

speaker: WARWICK
text: The King your father is disposed to sleep.

speaker:
THOMAS
               
              OF
               
              CLARENCE
text: Let us withdraw into the other room.

speaker: WARWICK
text: Will ’t please your Grace to go along with us?

speaker: PRINCE
text: No, I will sit and watch here by the King.

speaker: PRINCE
text: Why doth the crown lie there upon his pillow,

speaker: PRINCE
text: Being so troublesome a bedfellow?

speaker: PRINCE
text: O polished perturbation, golden care,

speaker: PRINCE
text: That keep’st the ports of slumber open wide

speaker: PRINCE
text: To many a watchful night! Sleep with it now;

speaker: PRINCE
text: Yet not so sound and half so deeply sweet

speaker: PRINCE
text: As he whose brow with homely biggen bound

speaker: PRINCE
text: Snores out the watch of night. O majesty,

speaker: PRINCE
text: When thou dost pinch thy bearer, thou dost sit

speaker: PRINCE
text: Like a rich armor worn in heat of day,

speaker: PRINCE
text: That scald’st with safety. By his gates of breath

speaker: PRINCE
text: There lies a downy feather which stirs not;

speaker: PRINCE
text: Did he suspire, that light and weightless down

speaker: PRINCE
text: Perforce must move. My gracious lord, my father,

speaker: PRINCE
text: This sleep is sound indeed. This is a sleep

speaker: PRINCE
text: That from this golden rigol hath divorced

speaker: PRINCE
text: So many English kings. Thy due from me

speaker: PRINCE
text: Is tears and heavy sorrows of the blood,

speaker: PRINCE
text: Which nature, love, and filial tenderness

speaker: PRINCE
text: Shall, O dear father, pay thee plenteously.

speaker: PRINCE
text: My due from thee is this imperial crown,

speaker: PRINCE
text: Which, as immediate from thy place and blood,

speaker: PRINCE
text:
Derives itself to me.
                 
                He
                 
                puts
                 
                on
                 
                the
                 
                crown
                .
               Lo, where it sits,

speaker: PRINCE
text: Which God shall guard. And, put the world’s whole strength

speaker: PRINCE
text: Into one giant arm, it shall not force

speaker: PRINCE
text: This lineal honor from me. This from thee

speaker: PRINCE
text: Will I to mine leave, as ’tis left to me.

speaker: KING
text: Warwick! Gloucester!

speaker: KING
text: Clarence!

speaker:
THOMAS
               
              OF
               
              CLARENCE
text: Doth the King call?

speaker: WARWICK
text: What would your Majesty? How fares your Grace?

speaker: KING
text: Why did you leave me here alone, my lords?

speaker:
THOMAS
               
              OF
               
              CLARENCE
text: We left the Prince my brother here, my liege,

speaker:
THOMAS
               
              OF
               
              CLARENCE
text: Who undertook to sit and watch by you.

speaker: KING
text: The Prince of Wales? Where is he? Let me see him.

speaker: KING
text: He is not here.

speaker: WARWICK
text: This door is open. He is gone this way.

speaker:
HUMPHREY
               
              OF
               
              GLOUCESTER
text: He came not through the chamber where we stayed.

speaker: KING
text: Where is the crown? Who took it from my pillow?

speaker: WARWICK
text: When we withdrew, my liege, we left it here.

speaker: KING
text: The Prince hath ta’en it hence. Go seek him out.

speaker: KING
text: Is he so hasty that he doth suppose my sleep my death?

speaker: KING
text: Find him, my Lord of Warwick. Chide him hither.

speaker: KING
text: This part of his conjoins with my disease

speaker: KING
text: And helps to end me. See, sons, what things you are,

speaker: KING
text: How quickly nature falls into revolt

speaker: KING
text: When gold becomes her object!

speaker: KING
text: For this the foolish overcareful fathers

speaker: KING
text: Have broke their sleep with thoughts,

speaker: KING
text: Their brains with care, their bones with industry.

speaker: KING
text: For this they have engrossèd and piled up

speaker: KING
text: The cankered heaps of strange-achievèd gold.

speaker: KING
text: For this they have been thoughtful to invest

speaker: KING
text: Their sons with arts and martial exercises—

speaker: KING
text: When, like the bee, tolling from every flower

speaker: KING
text: The virtuous sweets,

speaker: KING
text: Our thighs packed with wax, our mouths with honey,

speaker: KING
text: We bring it to the hive and, like the bees,

speaker: KING
text: Are murdered for our pains. This bitter taste

speaker: KING
text: Yields his engrossments to the ending father.

speaker: KING
text: Now where is he that will not stay so long

speaker: KING
text: Till his friend sickness hath determined me?

speaker: WARWICK
text: My lord, I found the Prince in the next room,

speaker: WARWICK
text: Washing with kindly tears his gentle cheeks,

speaker: WARWICK
text: With such a deep demeanor in great sorrow

speaker: WARWICK
text: That tyranny, which never quaffed but blood,

speaker: WARWICK
text: Would, by beholding him, have washed his knife

speaker: WARWICK
text: With gentle eyedrops. He is coming hither.

speaker: KING
text: But wherefore did he take away the crown?

speaker: KING
text: Lo where he comes.—Come hither to me, Harry.—

speaker: KING
text: Depart the chamber. Leave us here alone.

speaker: PRINCE
text: I never thought to hear you speak again.

speaker: KING
text: Thy wish was father, Harry, to that thought.

speaker: KING
text: I stay too long by thee; I weary thee.

speaker: KING
text: Dost thou so hunger for mine empty chair

speaker: KING
text: That thou wilt needs invest thee with my honors

speaker: KING
text: Before thy hour be ripe? O foolish youth,

speaker: KING
text: Thou seek’st the greatness that will overwhelm thee.

speaker: KING
text: Stay but a little, for my cloud of dignity

speaker: KING
text: Is held from falling with so weak a wind

speaker: KING
text: That it will quickly drop. My day is dim.

speaker: KING
text: Thou hast stol’n that which after some few hours

speaker: KING
text: Were thine without offense, and at my death

speaker: KING
text: Thou hast sealed up my expectation.

speaker: KING
text: Thy life did manifest thou loved’st me not,

speaker: KING
text: And thou wilt have me die assured of it.

speaker: KING
text: Thou hid’st a thousand daggers in thy thoughts,

speaker: KING
text: Whom thou hast whetted on thy stony heart

speaker: KING
text: To stab at half an hour of my life.

speaker: KING
text: What, canst thou not forbear me half an hour?

speaker: KING
text: Then get thee gone, and dig my grave thyself,

speaker: KING
text: And bid the merry bells ring to thine ear

speaker: KING
text: That thou art crownèd, not that I am dead.

speaker: KING
text: Let all the tears that should bedew my hearse

speaker: KING
text: Be drops of balm to sanctify thy head;

speaker: KING
text: Only compound me with forgotten dust.

speaker: KING
text: Give that which gave thee life unto the worms.

speaker: KING
text: Pluck down my officers, break my decrees,

speaker: KING
text: For now a time is come to mock at form.

speaker: KING
text: Harry the Fifth is crowned. Up, vanity,

speaker: KING
text: Down, royal state, all you sage councillors, hence,

speaker: KING
text: And to the English court assemble now,

speaker: KING
text: From every region, apes of idleness.

speaker: KING
text: Now, neighbor confines, purge you of your scum.

speaker: KING
text: Have you a ruffian that will swear, drink, dance,

speaker: KING
text: Revel the night, rob, murder, and commit

speaker: KING
text: The oldest sins the newest kind of ways?

speaker: KING
text: Be happy, he will trouble you no more.

speaker: KING
text: England shall double gild his treble guilt.

speaker: KING
text: England shall give him office, honor, might,

speaker: KING
text: For the fifth Harry from curbed license plucks

speaker: KING
text: The muzzle of restraint, and the wild dog

speaker: KING
text: Shall flesh his tooth on every innocent.

speaker: KING
text: O my poor kingdom, sick with civil blows!

speaker: KING
text: When that my care could not withhold thy riots,

speaker: KING
text: What wilt thou do when riot is thy care?

speaker: KING
text: O, thou wilt be a wilderness again,

speaker: KING
text: Peopled with wolves, thy old inhabitants.

speaker: PRINCE
text: O pardon me, my liege! But for my tears,

speaker: PRINCE
text: The moist impediments unto my speech,

speaker: PRINCE
text: I had forestalled this dear and deep rebuke

speaker: PRINCE
text: Ere you with grief had spoke and I had heard

speaker: PRINCE
text: The course of it so far. There is your crown,

speaker: PRINCE
text: And He that wears the crown immortally

speaker: PRINCE
text:
Long guard it yours.
                 
                He
                 
                kneels
                .
               If I affect it more

speaker: PRINCE
text: Than as your honor and as your renown,

speaker: PRINCE
text: Let me no more from this obedience rise,

speaker: PRINCE
text: Which my most inward true and duteous spirit

speaker: PRINCE
text: Teacheth this prostrate and exterior bending.

speaker: PRINCE
text: God witness with me, when I here came in

speaker: PRINCE
text: And found no course of breath within your Majesty,

speaker: PRINCE
text: How cold it struck my heart! If I do feign,

speaker: PRINCE
text: O, let me in my present wildness die

speaker: PRINCE
text: And never live to show th’ incredulous world

speaker: PRINCE
text: The noble change that I have purposèd.

speaker: PRINCE
text: Coming to look on you, thinking you dead,

speaker: PRINCE
text: And dead almost, my liege, to think you were,

speaker: PRINCE
text: I spake unto this crown as having sense,

speaker: PRINCE
text: And thus upbraided it: “The care on thee depending

speaker: PRINCE
text: Hath fed upon the body of my father;

speaker: PRINCE
text: Therefore thou best of gold art worst of gold.

speaker: PRINCE
text: Other, less fine in carat, is more precious,

speaker: PRINCE
text: Preserving life in med’cine potable;

speaker: PRINCE
text: But thou, most fine, most honored, most renowned,

speaker: PRINCE
text: Hast eat thy bearer up.” Thus, my most royal liege,

speaker: PRINCE
text: Accusing it, I put it on my head

speaker: PRINCE
text: To try with it, as with an enemy

speaker: PRINCE
text: That had before my face murdered my father,

speaker: PRINCE
text: The quarrel of a true inheritor.

speaker: PRINCE
text: But if it did infect my blood with joy

speaker: PRINCE
text: Or swell my thoughts to any strain of pride,

speaker: PRINCE
text: If any rebel or vain spirit of mine

speaker: PRINCE
text: Did with the least affection of a welcome

speaker: PRINCE
text: Give entertainment to the might of it,

speaker: PRINCE
text: Let God forever keep it from my head

speaker: PRINCE
text: And make me as the poorest vassal is

speaker: PRINCE
text: That doth with awe and terror kneel to it.

speaker: KING
text: O my son,

speaker: KING
text: God put it in thy mind to take it hence

speaker: KING
text: That thou mightst win the more thy father’s love,

speaker: KING
text: Pleading so wisely in excuse of it.

speaker: KING
text: Come hither, Harry, sit thou by my bed

speaker: KING
text: And hear, I think, the very latest counsel

speaker: KING
text: That ever I shall breathe.

speaker: KING
text: God knows, my son,

speaker: KING
text: By what bypaths and indirect crook’d ways

speaker: KING
text: I met this crown, and I myself know well

speaker: KING
text: How troublesome it sat upon my head.

speaker: KING
text: To thee it shall descend with better quiet,

speaker: KING
text: Better opinion, better confirmation,

speaker: KING
text: For all the soil of the achievement goes

speaker: KING
text: With me into the earth. It seemed in me

speaker: KING
text: But as an honor snatched with boist’rous hand,

speaker: KING
text: And I had many living to upbraid

speaker: KING
text: My gain of it by their assistances,

speaker: KING
text: Which daily grew to quarrel and to bloodshed,

speaker: KING
text: Wounding supposèd peace. All these bold fears

speaker: KING
text: Thou seest with peril I have answerèd,

speaker: KING
text: For all my reign hath been but as a scene

speaker: KING
text: Acting that argument. And now my death

speaker: KING
text: Changes the mood, for what in me was purchased

speaker: KING
text: Falls upon thee in a more fairer sort.

speaker: KING
text: So thou the garland wear’st successively.

speaker: KING
text: Yet though thou stand’st more sure than I could do,

speaker: KING
text: Thou art not firm enough, since griefs are green,

speaker: KING
text: And all my friends, which thou must make thy friends,

speaker: KING
text: Have but their stings and teeth newly ta’en out,

speaker: KING
text: By whose fell working I was first advanced

speaker: KING
text: And by whose power I well might lodge a fear

speaker: KING
text: To be again displaced; which to avoid,

speaker: KING
text: I cut them off and had a purpose now

speaker: KING
text: To lead out many to the Holy Land,

speaker: KING
text: Lest rest and lying still might make them look

speaker: KING
text: Too near unto my state. Therefore, my Harry,

speaker: KING
text: Be it thy course to busy giddy minds

speaker: KING
text: With foreign quarrels, that action, hence borne out,

speaker: KING
text: May waste the memory of the former days.

speaker: KING
text: More would I, but my lungs are wasted so

speaker: KING
text: That strength of speech is utterly denied me.

speaker: KING
text: How I came by the crown, O God forgive,

speaker: KING
text: And grant it may with thee in true peace live.

speaker: PRINCE
text: My gracious liege,

speaker: PRINCE
text: You won it, wore it, kept it, gave it me.

speaker: PRINCE
text: Then plain and right must my possession be,

speaker: PRINCE
text: Which I with more than with a common pain

speaker: PRINCE
text: ’Gainst all the world will rightfully maintain.

speaker: KING
text: Look, look, here comes my John of Lancaster.

speaker:
JOHN
               
              OF
               
              LANCASTER
text: Health, peace, and happiness to my royal father.

speaker: KING
text: Thou bring’st me happiness and peace, son John,

speaker: KING
text: But health, alack, with youthful wings is flown

speaker: KING
text: From this bare withered trunk. Upon thy sight

speaker: KING
text: My worldly business makes a period.

speaker: KING
text: Where is my Lord of Warwick?

speaker: PRINCE
text: My Lord of Warwick.

speaker: KING
text: Doth any name particular belong

speaker: KING
text: Unto the lodging where I first did swoon?

speaker: WARWICK
text: ’Tis called Jerusalem, my noble lord.

speaker: KING
text: Laud be to God! Even there my life must end.

speaker: KING
text: It hath been prophesied to me many years,

speaker: KING
text: I should not die but in Jerusalem,

speaker: KING
text: Which vainly I supposed the Holy Land.

speaker: KING
text: But bear me to that chamber; there I’ll lie.

speaker: KING
text: In that Jerusalem shall Harry die.
